participants.name: 제주대학교
participants.professional_persona: 제주대학교 환경공학과에서 박사 과정 중인 연구원으로, BK21 교육연구단의 지원을 받고 있습니다. 해양 미세플라스틱 오염도 조사 연구를 수행하며, 연구비로 분석 장비 임차 및 시약비를 충당하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 현장 시료 채취 및 푸리에 변환 적외선 분광법(FTIR)을 이용한 미세플라스틱 분석에 정통합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 제주 해양 환경 보전을 위한 과학적 데이터를 축적하여 정책 입안자들에게 실질적인 대안을 제시하는 환경 연구자가 되고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 광주 (afyvf0)
participants.professional_persona: 광주 소재 국립대학교 생명과학과 교수로, BK21 미래인재양성사업 생명공학 분야 교육연구그룹 책임자입니다. 유전자 치료 기술 개발에 주력하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 분자생물학 실험 기법과 동물 모델 연구에 숙달되어 있으며, 연구비 정산 및 보고서 작성 노하우가 뛰어납니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구그룹의 자체평가 보고서를 철저히 준비하여 컨설팅에서 좋은 평가를 받고, 궁극적으로 난치병 치료제 개발 연구를 세계적 수준으로 끌어올리는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.name: 제주대학교 (j96gho)
participants.professional_persona: 제주대학교 생물자원공학과 석사 과정 학생으로, BK21 교육연구단에서 감귤 가공 부산물을 활용한 기능성 소재 연구를 하고 있습니다. 지역 특산물을 연구 소재로 삼아 제주 농업 발전에 기여하고 싶습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 생리활성 물질 추출 및 분석(HPLC, LC-MS) 기술을 보유했으며, 항산화 실험 경험이 풍부합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 제주도 내 식품 연구소 또는 농업기술센터에 취업하여 지역 자원 활용 연구를 계속하고 싶습니다.

participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-k7obh350

participants.skills_and_expertise: 계량경제학 모델링과 빅데이터 기반 경제 예측에 강점이 있으며, STATA와 R을 능숙하게 다룹니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 제주연구원이나 한국은행에 입사하여 지역경제 정책 수립에 기여하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 인천 (n5ai2g)
participants.professional_persona: 인천 소재 사립대학교 컴퓨터공학과 부교수로, 7년째 BK21 교육연구그룹을 이끌고 있습니다. 클라우드 컴퓨팅과 빅데이터 분석을 연구합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 소프트웨어 아키텍처 설계와 연구개발 프로젝트 관리 능력을 갖추었고, 대학원생 대상 온라인 인권교육 콘텐츠를 직접 개발한 경험이 있습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a BK21 education research group in biotechnology at a national university in Gwangju, I base my estimates on the policy context and my group's typical allocation. The total funding for education research groups in 2022 is stated as 355,151 million KRW, which I take as the total for all groups, not just Hanyang University. For Hanyang University specifically, I estimate a proportional share based on the number of groups (577 total) and typical field budgets. My group's experience suggests that for a mid-sized university like Hanyang, the annual budget for BK21 is around 1.5-2 billion KRW, but the policy states 355,151 million KRW total for all groups, so Hanyang's portion might be around 2-3 billion KRW. However, the prediction field asks for total funding allocated to Hanyang University BK21 program, which includes both research group budgets and graduate innovation support. The graduate innovation support is 52,896 million KRW total across 20 universities, so Hanyang might get about 2.6 billion KRW from that. Combining, I estimate total funding for Hanyang at around 5.6 billion KRW, but the policy context states the total for education research groups is 355,151 million KRW, so I'll use that as the total for all groups, and for Hanyang, I'll estimate based on typical group size. Actually, the prediction field is for Hanyang University, so I need to estimate Hanyang's share. Given 577 groups and 68 universities, Hanyang likely has multiple groups. I'll assume Hanyang has about 10 groups, each with an average budget of 600 million KRW, totaling 6 billion KRW, plus graduate innovation support of about 2.6 billion KRW, total 8.6 billion KRW. But the policy states total funding for all groups is 355,151 million KRW, so that's 355 billion, which is for all 577 groups. Hanyang's share might be around 10-15 billion KRW. I'll estimate 10 billion KRW for simplicity. For graduate scholarship slots, based on stipend levels (master's 700K, doctoral 1.3M, doctoral candidate 1M), and assuming an average stipend of 1M KRW per month, a budget of 10 billion KRW for scholarships (part of total) could support about 833 student-years, but that's too high. More realistically, my group has about 20-30 students, and Hanyang might have 10 groups, so 200-300 students. I'll estimate 120 slots as a conservative number for the 2022 window. For postdoc positions, with minimum salary 3M per month, each postdoc costs 36M per year. With a budget of maybe 500 million for postdocs, that supports about 14 positions. I'll estimate 15.
evidence: The policy states total education research group budget is 355,151 million KRW for 577 groups, implying an average of about 615 million KRW per group, but Hanyang likely has multiple groups and additional innovation funds.
Graduate stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW per month) and postdoc minimum salary (3M per month) constrain the number of slots based on available budget.
Eligibility criteria limit the number of education research groups per university by field and region, so Hanyang's slots are proportional to its number of approved groups.
My experience as a group leader suggests typical group sizes of 10-20 graduate students and 1-2 postdocs, scaling to university level yields around 120 graduate slots and 15 postdoc positions.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student at Jeju National University, I see Hanyang University's BK21 allocation from a regional perspective. The total budget for education research groups is 355,151 million KRW for 577 groups, averaging about 615 million KRW per group. Hanyang likely has around 10-12 groups, so roughly 6-7 billion KRW for group budgets. Adding graduate innovation support (52,896 million KRW across 20 universities, Hanyang's share maybe 2.6 billion) gives about 8-10 billion KRW total. I estimate 8 billion KRW as a reasonable midpoint. For graduate slots, with average stipend ~1M KRW/month per student, a scholarship budget of about 1.2 billion KRW (15% of total) supports 100 slots (100*12M=1.2B). Postdoc positions: each costs 36M/year minimum salary, so 12 positions cost 432M KRW, fitting within the remaining budget. Other participants' estimates (120 slots, 15 postdocs) seem slightly high given Hanyang's typical group size and budget constraints; my experience suggests 100 slots and 12 postdocs are more realistic for a single year.

Postdoc minimum salary is 3M KRW/month (36M/year); 12 positions cost 432M KRW, a plausible allocation within the remaining budget.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a BK21 education research group in computer engineering at a private university in Incheon, I estimate Hanyang University's total BK21 funding for 2022 at about 10 billion KRW. This is based on the policy context stating 355,151 million KRW total for 577 education research groups, averaging ~615 million per group. Hanyang likely hosts around 10-12 groups, giving ~6-7 billion for group budgets, plus a share of the 52,896 million KRW graduate innovation support (about 2.6 billion for Hanyang as one of 20 universities), totaling ~8.6-9.6 billion. I round to 10 billion to account for some groups having larger budgets due to field or performance. For graduate slots, with an average monthly stipend of about 1 million KRW (master's 700K, doctoral 1.3M, doctoral candidate 1M), a scholarship budget of roughly 1.44 billion KRW (about 14.4% of total) supports 120 slots (120 * 12M = 1.44B). This is consistent with my group's experience: we have about 20-30 students, and Hanyang with 10 groups would have 200-300 students, but not all are funded; 120 slots seems plausible for the 2022 window. For postdoc positions, each costs at least 36 million KRW per year (3M/month minimum salary). A budget of about 540 million KRW (5.4% of total) supports 15 positions (15 * 36M = 540M). Other participants' estimates (8 billion, 100 slots, 12 postdocs) are reasonable but slightly conservative; my experience with a large private university suggests Hanyang's allocation is likely higher due to its size and research output. I disagree with the 8 billion estimate because Hanyang is a major research university with strong engineering and science programs, likely hosting more than the average number of groups and receiving additional innovation funds.

Postdoc minimum salary of 3 million KRW/month (36M/year) means 15 positions cost 540 million KRW (5.4% of total), a reasonable allocation for a research-intensive university like Hanyang.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of a BK21 education research group in biotechnology at a national university in Gwangju, I reason from my experience with the program's operations. The total funds disbursed to Hanyang University should match the allocated amount of 77,830,200,000 KRW, as the two-installment schedule (70% in March, 30% in September) is a standard procedure that I have seen executed reliably in my own group. Evaluation panels are convened for the annual evaluation, mid-term evaluation, and compliance checks, totaling 3 meetings per year.
